documents.title: On the Subject of Wires
documents.flavorText: Wires are the lifeblood of electronics! Wait, no, electricity is the lifeblood. Wires are more like the arteries. The veins? No matter...
rules: A wire module can have 3-6 wires on it.
Only the one correct wire needs to be cut to disarm the module.
Wire ordering begins with the first on the top.

4_wires: Multiple red and odd serial? Cut last red.
Last yellow and no red? Cut 1st.
5_wires: Last black and odd serial? Cut 4th.
One red and multiple yellow? Cut 1st.
6_wires: No yellow and odd serial? Cut 3rd.
One yellow and multiple white? Cut 4th.

documents.title: On the Subject of The Button
documents.flavorText: You might think that a button telling you to press it is pretty straightforward. That's the kind of thinking that gets people exploded.
rules: Indicators (like 'Lit CAR') can be found on the SIDES of the bomb. FLIP the bomb to check all edgework.
Follow these rules in order. Perform the first action that applies (DO NOT Release YET, SCROLL DOWN):

actions: Three or more Batteries and Lit FRK? Tap.

releasing.title: Releasing a Held Button
releasing.instruction: If holding, check the colored strip color:
strip_colors: Blue strip? Release when the timer has a 4 in any position.
White strip? Release when the timer has a 1 in any position.
Yellow strip? Release when the timer has a 5 in any position.
Other? Release when the timer has a 1 in any position.

documents.title: On the Subject of Keypads
documents.flavorText: I'm not sure what these symbols are, but I suspect they have something to do with occult.

content.text: Find the one column below containing all four symbols.
Press them in order from top to bottom.

documents.title: On the Subject of Simon Says
documents.flavorText: This is like one of those toys you played with as a kid... except this one is a knockoff.
rules: One of the four colored buttons will flash.
Using the correct table below, press the button with the corresponding color.
The sequence will lengthen by one each time you correctly enter a sequence.

documents.title: On the Subject of Complicated Wires
documents.flavorText: These wires aren't like the others. Some have stripes! ... The good news is that we've found a concise set of instructions... Maybe too concise...
rules: Look at each wire: there is an LED above the wire and a space for a '*' symbol below the wire.
For each wire/LED/symbol combination, use the Venn diagram below to decide whether or not to cut the wire.

legend.S: Cut the wire if the last digit of the serial number is even
legend.P: Cut the wire if the last digit of the serial number is even (Field Mod)
legend.B: Cut the wire if the bomb has two or more batteries

documents.title: On the Subject of Wire Sequences
documents.flavorText: It's hard to say how this mechanism works... there must have been an easier way to manage nine wires.

content.text: The most important part of this module is tracking the **OCCURRENCE** of each wire color. This is a cumulative count that does NOT reset between panels.
**Definition:** An 'occurrence' is the total number of times a wire of a specific color has appeared in the module so far, starting from the first panel.
**Example:** If Panel 1 has two Red wires, the top one is 'Red Occurrence #1' and the bottom one is 'Red Occurrence #2'. If Panel 2 then has another Red wire, that wire is 'Red Occurrence #3'.
1. For each wire, note its **color** and its **occurrence number** (how many of that color have you seen in total?).
2. Note which letter (**A, B, or C**) the wire is connected to on the right side.
3. Consult the tables below. If the letter is listed for that specific color and occurrence, **CUT THE WIRE**.
4. Use the ◀ and ▶ buttons to move between the 4 panels. You MUST check every wire in every panel.
5. Once all required wires are cut, go to the final panel and click the checkmark (**✔**) to finish. Missing a required wire will trigger a STRIKE.
